title: Delicious Dry Greek Gigantes Plaki
description: A light and flavorful Greek dish featuring large beans in a sweet and tangy tomato sauce. This recipe is perfect for a weeknight dinner or as a snack.

ingredients.name: gigantes beans
ingredients.notes: large white cannellini beans can be substituted if gigantes beans are not available

ingredients.name: canned crushed tomatoes
ingredients.notes: diced tomatoes can be used instead of crushed tomatoes

ingredients.name: canned water
ingredients.notes: from the crushed tomatoes can, or water as needed

ingredients.name: salt
ingredients.notes: use a salt substitute for a low-sodium option

ingredients.name: feta cheese
ingredients.notes: crumbly, can be omitted for a dairy-free option

instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and sauté for 5-7 minutes or until it starts to soften.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes or until fragrant.
Add the tomato paste and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Add the crushed tomatoes, water, caster sugar, salt, and black pepper. Stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 10-15 minutes or until the sauce has thickened slightly.
Add the gigantes beans and feta cheese (if using) to the saucepan. Stir to combine.
Reduce heat to low and cook for an additional 5-10 minutes or until the flavors have melded together and the beans are coated in the sauce.
Serve hot, garnished with additional feta cheese and a sprinkle of parsley if desired.
